title: cassia littoralis
intro: Cassia littoralis, also known as the coastal cassia, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family of legumes. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Cassia littoralis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and aid in digestion, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent the growth of certain microorganisms. Cassia littoralis can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in herbal formulations to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
